I agree with the initial assessment that option B, which is "Lordosis," correctly identifies the normal curvature typically found in the lumbar spine. The term specifically refers to the natural inward curve in this area, distinguishing it from other conditions such as scoliosis (abnormal lateral spinal curvatures), kyphosis (excessive outward thoracic curve). This inward curve helps distribute weight evenly across the lower back vertebrae during standing and walking activities.
